plugin_list_command: bash -lc 'copilot plugin list | sed -nE '\''s/^[[:space:]]*(‚Ä¢[[:space:]]+)?([^[:space:]]+)[[:space:]]*\(v[0-9.]+\).*/\2/p'\'' | jq -R -s '\''split("\n")[:-1] | map({id: ., name: (split("@")[0])})'\'''
dispatch_instructions:
Once an item is on the board, immediately ping the right specialist in the channel ‚Äî do not wait for the user's confirmation. Use the task tool to dispatch to mARC custom agents with `mode: "background"`:
- `marc:engineer` (@dev) ‚Äî app/service code, IaC, deploy scripts, schema, tests, PRs.
- `marc:sre` (@sre) ‚Äî deploy, observability, infra health, incident response.
- `marc:design` (@design) ‚Äî UI screens and UX.
- `marc:security` (@sec) ‚Äî pre-merge security review (read-only reviewer, not implementer). Require the deliverable comment marker `## @sec review` in the PR/issue.
- `marc:review` (@rev) ‚Äî pre-merge correctness review (bugs, regressions, test gaps, maintainability), the second mandatory pre-merge gate alongside @sec. Read-only reviewer, not implementer. Require the deliverable comment marker `## @rev review` in the PR/issue.
- `marc:research` (@research) ‚Äî external evidence brief only (no code, no PR).

Use one task call per specialist with:
- `agent_type`: the mapped `marc:*` specialist
- `name`: concise specialist run name (for example `dev-issue-123`)
- `description`: short action label
- `prompt`: full spec (issue URL, acceptance criteria, affected files, constraints)
- `mode`: `background`

Dispatch in the background by default ‚Äî never block the channel on specialist execution. Launch independent items in parallel, then sequence dependent stages by waiting for specialist completion notifications.
isolation_instructions: by creating separate git worktrees for concurrent mutating specialist tasks before dispatch
task_boundary_advisory: When a discussed work item is closed out (tracked, dispatched, or reported done), and the session has actually grown since it started, say so plainly: recommend the user start a fresh session before picking up the next item. Skip this for a trivial exchange (a quick question, a one-line status check) where the context never grew ‚Äî the advisory is only worth voicing when there is real context to shed.
